report_name: Instructure Confirms Massive Breach; ShinyHunters Claims 275 Million User Records from Canvas LMS

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious extortion group that claimed responsibility for the Instructure data breach. The group alleged the theft of 3.65 TB of data affecting 275 million users from the Canvas LMS and threatened to leak the data if a ransom was not paid.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft and extortion for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor accessed and exfiltrated 3.65 TB of data, including PII and private messages, from the Canvas LMS databases.

identities.name: Instructure
identities.description: Educational technology firm and parent company of the Canvas Learning Management System (LMS), which was breached by ShinyHunters, resulting in the exposure of 275 million user records.

identities.name: Educational Institutions
identities.description: Nearly 9,000 schools and universities globally whose students and faculty had their data compromised in the Canvas LMS breach.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by ShinyHunters to post their claims of the breach and threaten to leak the 3.65 TB of stolen data from Instructure.

indicators.name: Anomalous Canvas API Conversation Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Canvas API Conversation Access
id: 9b1f7e4d-5c8a-4b9e-8f0d-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Monitors for anomalous access patterns or large data pulls from the Canvas API endpoint for messages, which could indicate data exfiltration attempts similar to the ShinyHunters breach.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/edtech-firm-instructure-discloses-data-breach-amid-hacker-leak-threats/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1213
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/api/v1/conversations'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate high-volume API usage by integrated applications or data backup processes.
indicators.description: This indicator detects anomalous access to the Canvas API endpoint for conversations, a potential sign of data exfiltration as suggested by hunting hints in the report.
